pick.rationale: Arkansas holds a substantial advantage in this 4/13 matchup, ranking 17th-20th nationally in efficiency metrics with an elite top-5 offense that should overwhelm Hawai'i's mid-major defense. The Razorbacks' SEC pedigree and John Calipari's tournament experience provide significant advantages over the Rainbow Warriors, who despite winning the Big West Tournament and having prior 13-seed upset history (beating Cal in 2016), face a considerable talent and competition level gap. While 13-seeds pull upsets approximately 20% of the time and Hawai'i enters with momentum, Arkansas's +25.43 adjusted efficiency margin and balanced profile with top-50 defense should prove decisive in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Arkansas vs Hawai'i 4/13 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas favored based on elite offensive efficiency, SEC pedigree, and substantial efficiency gap over Big West champion Hawai'i
reasoningStep.evidence: Arkansas ranks 17th (KenPom) and 20th (Torvik) with top-5 offense (127.7 adjO) and 50th-ranked defense, while Hawai'i lacks comparable efficiency metrics as a Big West automatic qualifier. The Razorbacks' 25-8 SEC record and Calipari's tournament experience contrast with Hawai'i's 24-8 mid-major profile, though the Rainbow Warriors have historical 13-seed upset precedent from 2016 when they beat California.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://bigwest.org/news/2026/3/15/mens-basketball-big-west-champion-hawai-i-receives-13-seed-and-draws-arkansas-in-march-madness-first-round.aspx and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round - The Big West Skip To Main Content Big West Conference Open/Close Scoreboard Main Big West On Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball 3/15/2026 3:45:00 PM Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round Story Links Interactive Bracket Hawai’i, winner of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Men’s Basketball Championship, is going dancing and headed to Portland, Ore. in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament. The Rainbow Warriors earned the No. 13 seed and will face No. 4 seed Arkansas of the SEC in the first round of the West Region on Thursday, March 19 at the Moda Center. The advancing team will move on to the second round to face the winner of No. 5 seed Wisconsin and No. 12 seed High Point, Saturday. We’re dancing! ?? #ManaOhana x #GoBows pic.twitter.com/d1p1UPrfAT — HAWAII BASKETBALL (@HawaiiMBB) March 15, 2026 Hawai’i (24-8) earned its place in the big dance as the conference’s automatic qualifier after defeating UC Irvine 71-64 in the title game of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship, Saturday night at Lee’s Family Forum in Henderson, Nev. Arkansas (26-8) is also an automatic qualifier after winning the SEC Tournament with an 86-75 win over Wisconsin as the third seed. Hawai’i is making its sixth NCAA Tournament appearance overall (1972, 1994, 2001, 2002, 2016), and second representing The Big West and under head coach Eran Ganot . The last time the Rainbow Warriors made the NCAA Tournament was in 2016, when they were also a No. 13 seed and upset Cal in the first round. It will be the first time since Hawai’i has faced Arkansas since 1977. The Rainbow Warriors are 0-2 all-time against Arkansas. As a team, Hawai‘i is sound defensively, ranking in the top 25 in the country in defensive rebounds per game (28.2 DRPG) and 3-point percentage defense (.304 Opp 3PP). The Rainbow Warriors also top 25 in free throws made per game (18.6 FTM/G) and free throw attempts per game (25.1 FTA/G). Hawai’i is led by senior center Isaac Johnson , an All-Big West First Team selection and the championship’s Most Outstanding Player, who averages 14.1 points on a .503 field goal percentage and 5.8 rebounds per game. Graduate guard Dre Bullock , an All-Big West Second Team selection, adds 13.5 points and 5.8 boards per outing and senior forward Harry Rouhliadeff pitches in 10.8 points and 5.0 rebounds per contest. Both Bullock and Rouhliadeff joined Johnson on the All-Tournament team. Related Stories and Videos Related Stories 03.17.26 No. 4 Seed UC Irvine Falls in First Round of NIT 03.17.26 NABC All-Pacific District Teams Features Five Big West Selections 03.15.26 UC Irvine Featured in 2026 NIT Bracket as No. 4 Seed 03.14.26 No. 2 Seed Hawai'i Wins 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship 03.13.26 No. 2 Seed Hawai'i Pulls Away in Second Half to Move on to Big West Championship Final Related Video

Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round - The Big West Skip To Main Content Big West Conference Open/Close Scoreboard Main Big West On Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball 3/15/2026 3:45:00 PM Big West Champion Hawai’i Receives No. 13 Seed and Draws Arkansas in March Madness First Round Story Links Interactive Bracket Hawai’i, winner of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Men’s Basketball Championship, is going dancing and headed to Portland, Ore. in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament. The Rainbow Warriors earned the No. 13 seed and will face No. 4 seed Arkansas of the SEC in the first round of the West Region on Thursday, March 19 at the Moda Center. The advancing team will move on to the second round to face the winner of No. 5 seed Wisconsin and No. 12 seed High Point, Saturday. We’re dancing! ?? #ManaOhana x #GoBows pic.twitter.com/d1p1UPrfAT — HAWAII BASKETBALL (@HawaiiMBB) March 15, 2026 Hawai’i (24-8) earned its place in the big dance as the conference’s automatic qualifier after defeating UC Irvine 71-64 in the title game of the 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship, Saturday night at Lee’s Family Forum in Henderson, Nev. Arkansas (26-8) is also an automatic qualifier after winning the SEC Tournament with an 86-75 win over Wisconsin as the third seed. Hawai’i is making its sixth NCAA Tournament appearance overall (1972, 1994, 2001, 2002, 2016), and second representing The Big West and under head coach Eran Ganot . The last time the Rainbow Warriors made the NCAA Tournament was in 2016, when they were also a No. 13 seed and upset Cal in the first round. It will be the first time since Hawai’i has faced Arkansas since 1977. The Rainbow Warriors are 0-2 all-time against Arkansas. As a team, Hawai‘i is sound defensively, ranking in the top 25 in the country in defensive rebounds per game (28.2 DRPG) and 3-point percentage defense (.304 Opp 3PP). The Rainbow Warriors also top 25 in free throws made per game (18.6 FTM/G) and free throw attempts per game (25.1 FTA/G). Hawai’i is led by senior center Isaac Johnson , an All-Big West First Team selection and the championship’s Most Outstanding Player, who averages 14.1 points on a .503 field goal percentage and 5.8 rebounds per game. Graduate guard Dre Bullock , an All-Big West Second Team selection, adds 13.5 points and 5.8 boards per outing and senior forward Harry Rouhliadeff pitches in 10.8 points and 5.0 rebounds per contest. Both Bullock and Rouhliadeff joined Johnson on the All-Tournament team. Related Stories and Videos Related Stories 03.17.26 No. 4 Seed UC Irvine Falls in First Round of NIT 03.17.26 NABC All-Pacific District Teams Features Five Big West Selections 03.15.26 UC Irvine Featured in 2026 NIT Bracket as No. 4 Seed 03.14.26 No. 2 Seed Hawai'i Wins 2026 Credit Union 1 Big West Championship 03.13.26 No. 2 Seed Hawai'i Pulls Away in Second Half to Move on to Big West Championship Final Related Video
